name: Glaceed Sweet Potatoes B1
recipeInstructions:
----------------------
Batter Preparation:
1. All-purpose flour
2. Cornstarch
3. Egg
4. Baking powder, 1/2 teaspoon
5. Corn oil, 2 tablespoons
6. Water
1. In a large bowl, mix the flour and cornstarch.
2. Gradually add the egg, baking powder, and reserved 2 tablespoons of corn oil to the dry ingredients.
3. Slowly incorporate water until the mixture reaches the consistency of pancake batter.
4. Peel the sweet potatoes and dice them into 1-inch squares.
5. Preheat a wok or frying pan with enough oil to submerge the potatoes.
6. Heat the oil to 350°F (175°C).
7. Dip the diced sweet potatoes into the batter, ensuring they are well-coated.
8. Deep fry the potatoes in batches for about 3 minutes until golden brown.
9. Remove the fried potatoes and drain on a paper towel-lined plate.
10. In a separate pan or saucepan, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of corn oil over medium heat.
11. Stir in the sugar and continuously whisk until it turns into a light brown syrup.
12. Place the fried sweet potatoes into the syrup to coat evenly.
13. Allow them to cool slightly before transferring to a hot, greased serving platter.
Temperature: HOT
Effort: AVERAGE
Time: 15 minutes
